mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary policy, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days as lower discount rates boost equity valuations.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 7.0% signals tightening labor market conditions, which historically correlate with positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days due to improved corporate earnings prospects.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in periods with UNRATE < 7.0% are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) indicates a robust labor market, which drives S&P 500 returns positively over 5 trading days through enhanced consumer spending and corporate profitability.
failure_condition: The interaction between PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to produce positive 5-day returns in 60% of cases or the joint signal's IC falls below 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Rising residential construction (HOUST) and consumer spending (RSAFS) during a demand expansion phase signal strong economic activity, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days via elevated corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: The combined HOUST-RSAFS signal fails to generate positive 60-day returns in 50% of cases or the IC drops below 0.08.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 99.0 indicates strengthening supply-side dynamics, which historically correlate with positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through improved corporate margins and economic confidence.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in INDPRO > 99.0 periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC drops below 0.07.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 2.5% triggers hawkish monetary policy expectations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as investors discount higher discount rates and potential rate hikes.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in CPI > 2.5% periods are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC rises above -0.05.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly gain in payrolls (PAYEMS) exceeding 100k signals strong labor market health, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days through increased consumer spending and corporate investment.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in PAYEMS > 100k periods are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) or the signal's IC drops below 0.06.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of low FEDFUNDS and rising CPI (CPIAUCSL) indicates a central bank's accommodative stance amid inflation, which historically correlates with positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days through mixed monetary conditions.
failure_condition: The FEDFUNDS-CPIAUCSL interaction fails to produce positive 60-day returns in 50% of cases or the joint signal's IC falls below 0.07.
